title: Annual report on the Export of Objects of Cultural Interest 2024 to 25
body: "The 70th annual report by the Reviewing Committee on the Export of Works of Art and Objects of Cultural Interest. The report covers the period 1 May 2024 to 30 April 2025, in which 20 cases were considered by the Reviewing Committee. In 18 cases, a decision on the export licence application was deferred by the Secretary of State following the recommendation of the Committee on the basis that the item qualified as a national treasure. Two items were found not to meet the criteria for national treasure status.  6 items were bought by institutions or individuals in the UK:    A History of the Birds of Europe, H. E. Dresser Coin found in UK soil, a new IronAge Ruler The Delilah Project: working papers, Alan Turing A needlework sampler of MenaiBridge, Anglesey by Mary Anne Hughes, age 11 The Sutton Scotney Iron Age Brooch Early Anglo Saxon Gold and Garnet Cloisonné Panel, trapezoidal form   9 items were or can be exported.  3 were still under deferral at the time of writing this report. "
